senators.name: Bernie Sanders
senators.nerdReason: B.A. in Political Science, not STEM, but consistently cites CBO, BLS, and peer-reviewed economic research on the Senate floor. Strong advocate for NIH and NSF funding. Championed evidence-based Medicare for All proposals grounded in health-economics literature. Pushed for climate action citing IPCC reports. Record is mixed by the rubric because education is non-technical, but application of data in policy is above average.
education: B.A. Political Science, University of Chicago

senators.name: Peter Welch
senators.notNerdReason: J.D. from UC Berkeley and B.A. from Holy Cross — legal and liberal arts background with no STEM credentials. Uses data selectively on energy and climate policy but does not have a track record of deeply citing research or pushing evidence-based frameworks. Solid on supporting science funding but primarily a traditional legislative dealmaker.
education: J.D., University of California, Berkeley School of Law
B.A. History, College of the Holy Cross

VT-01.name: Becca Balint
VT-01.nerdReason: M.Ed. from Harvard and B.A. from Smith College — education-focused background without STEM or hard-policy research credentials. Former middle and high school history teacher. Advocates for evidence-based education policy and climate action but has limited record of citing primary research or data-driven legislative frameworks. Supportive of science funding but not a policy wonk by the rubric's standards.
education: M.A. History, University of Massachusetts Amherst
M.Ed., Harvard Graduate School of Education
B.A., Smith College (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa)
